tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    Plot a fault tree from illustrate() output

    plot.Rd

    Builds a ggplot of the fault tree using the nodes, edges, and gate polygons returned by illustrate. Gate nodes (and, or, top) are drawn as polygons with outlines that follow their shape; other nodes (e.g. basic events) are drawn as circle polygons in the same data coordinate system, so their size stays visually consistent relative to the gates. Nodes and gates are labelled using the event column from x$nodes.

    plot(
  x,
  type_col = "type",
  gate_types = c("and", "or", "top"),
  edge_linewidth = 1,
  gate_linewidth = 0.8,
  gate_alpha = 1,
  point_radius = NULL,
  point_n = 100,
  point_linewidth = 0.8,
  outline_colour = "black",
  gate_fill = NULL,
  coord_fixed = TRUE,
  theme_void = TRUE,
  expand = 0.2,
  normalize = TRUE,
  basic_radius_ratio = 0.18,
  ...
)

    Arguments

    x

    A list returned by illustrate(..., type = "both") or illustrate(..., type = "all"), with elements nodes, edges, and gates.

    type_col

    Character. Name of the column in x$nodes that holds node type (e.g. "top", "and", "or", "not"). Default is "type".

    gate_types

    Character vector. Node types that are drawn as gate polygons. Default is c("and", "or", "top").

    edge_linewidth

    Numeric. Line width for edges. Default 1.

    gate_linewidth

    Numeric. Outline width for gate polygons. Default 0.8.

    gate_alpha

    Numeric. Fill opacity for gate polygons (0–1). Default 1.

    point_radius

    Numeric or NULL. Radius of basic-event circles in data units. If NULL (default), it is estimated from gate sizes (see basic_radius_ratio). Set explicitly (e.g. 0.1) for full control when auto sizing is too small or too big.

    point_n

    Integer. Number of vertices used to draw each basic-event circle polygon. Default 100.

    point_linewidth

    Numeric. Outline width for basic-event circles. Default 0.8.

    outline_colour

    Character. Colour for gate polygon and basic-event outlines. Default "black".

    gate_fill

    Named character vector of fill colours for gate types (and, or, top) and optionally "basic event" for leaf nodes. If NULL (default), the viridis discrete palette is used. When provided, scale_fill_manual is used with these values.

    coord_fixed

    Logical. If TRUE (default), use equal aspect ratio so shapes are not stretched.

    theme_void

    Logical. If TRUE (default), remove axes and panel.

    expand

    Numeric. Fixed padding (in data units) added to the view limits on each side. Default 0.2. For trees with few nodes, the function automatically tightens this to 0.1.

    normalize

    Logical. If TRUE (default), scale and center node, edge, and gate coordinates so the plot extent is standardized across different trees.

    basic_radius_ratio

    Numeric. Used only when point_radius = NULL. Basic-event radius is set to this fraction of the median gate extent (each gate's extent is half the sum of its x and y span). Default 0.18. Larger values give bigger circles; smaller values give smaller circles.

    ...

    Ignored (for compatibility with generic plot).

    Value

    A ggplot object.

    Details

    To avoid pixelated output when saving, use ggsave() with an explicit dpi (e.g. ggsave("plot.png", p, dpi = 300)). Saving from the RStudio Plots pane or at a small size often produces low-resolution images.

    Basic-event circle size when point_radius = NULL: the function takes each gate polygon's extent (half of x-span + y-span), then uses the median of those extents times basic_radius_ratio. So circle size follows a typical gate size. It can still look too small or too big when gate sizes vary a lot (e.g. one large top gate and many small gates), or when the layout is very compact or very spread out. In those cases, set point_radius explicitly (e.g. 0.08 or 0.15) or adjust basic_radius_ratio.

    Examples

    data(it_security_nodes, it_security_edges, package = "tidyfault")
ill <- illustrate(nodes = it_security_nodes, edges = it_security_edges, type = "both")
p <- plot(ill)
if (FALSE)  ggsave("fault_tree.png", p, dpi = 300)  # \dontrun{}

    Contents

    Developed by Timothy Fraser, Jingyao Tong.
